semanticCore: Resembling, matching, imitating, copying, or mimicking

keyDerivations.meaning: To resemble, match, copy, imitate, or mimic another's statement/action

figurativeOrRareUsages: ذَٰلِكَ قَوْلُهُمْ بِأَفْوَاهِهِمْ ۖ يُضَاهِئُونَ قَوْلَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ قَبْلُ (9:30): That is their statement by their mouths; they imitate/match the saying of those who disbelieved before.
summaryEn: The root signifies imitating or mimicking (Dah'), accusing the Jews and Christians of copying the theological formulas of ancient polytheists by claiming Ezra or Jesus as sons of God.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر المشابهة والمضاهاة ومطابقة الأقوال والتقليد الأعمى للباطل (ضها)، مبيناً تشابه عقائد أهل الكتاب المنحرفة مع وثنيات الأمم السابقة.
rawText: ضَهْيَأٌ and ضَهْيَأَةٌ: see art. ضهيأ. مُضَاهَأَةٌ 3 is syn. with مُضَاهَاةٌ, (K, TA, [the و before المُضاهاةُ in the CK should be erased,]) i. q. مُشَاكَلَةٌ: (S, O, Msb, TA:) you say, ضَاهَأْتُهُ and ضَاهَيْتُهُ, ('Eyn, S, * O, * Msb, * TA,) i. e. I resembled him; syn. شَابَهْتُهُ: ('Eyn, TA:) [or I conformed with him:] or ضَاهَأَهُ, inf. n. as above, signifies he imitated him; syn. عَارَضَهُ and بَارَاهُ; and one says also, ضَاهَيْتُهُ, inf. n. مُضَاهَاةٌ: (Msb:) the verb is read both with and without ء in the Kur [ix. 30]. (O, Msb, TA.) === ضَاهَا بِهِ, (A 'Obeyd, TA,) [or ضَاهَأَهُ, like ضَاهَاهُ, mentioned in art. ضهى,] inf. n. as above, (K,) He (a man) was, or became, gentle, tender, or courteous, or he acted, or behaved, gently, &c., with him, or to him. (A 'Obeyd, K, * TA.)
